title: Time Warp
result_description: You snap your fingers and time accelerates: you stay normal speed in the foreground while the clouds/sky behind you race past in a fast time-lapse.

inputs.name: action_clip
inputs.what_to_film: On a locked-off, exposure-locked camera with a moving-cloud sky, do your action and snap your fingers, then let the camera keep rolling for several minutes to capture a long time-lapse of the same scene.

capture_requirements.note: one continuous take: action + snap, then long static hold; the slower the clouds, the longer you record

params.note: two stacked copies sharing identical timing; bottom = foreground cutout, top/base = sky time-lapse
slide so both copies line up exactly
split at the finger snap so the time-lapse starts there
speed up the post-snap section a lot to make the clouds fly; tune to taste (creator used roughly 80x)
PRO: brush over yourself/foreground to cut out from the sky
FREE alternative to Custom Removal: cover the foreground so only the sky speeds up
blend the mask edge along the treeline
add a second mask to fill in trees the horizontal mask misses; small feather
trim the sped-up end so foreground and sky end together

result.description: Foreground stays normal while the sky rockets past in time-lapse after the snap.
success_criteria: Clouds visibly accelerate at the snap
Foreground subject unaffected by the speed change
Cutout/mask edge hidden along the treeline
